titleStmt.title: SII 26.285: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 285. (A.R. No. 270 of 1909.) TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the same shrine.

p.hi: Tribhº Rājarājadēva III: Year 3, Makara (Tai) śu, 4, Sunday, Mūla: Irregular = c. 1218-19 A.D.

summary.p: This inscription records the gift of two sandhyā lamps to the temple of the god Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyār of Tirukkachchūr occurring in No. 284 above, one by Kālaimālai-pāgamvaittāṉ Villavanāḻvāṉ and another by Kaḷariyakkaḷattuḻāṉ Oṟṟikoṇḍāṉ Irāśarāśadēvaṉ of the same village. A sum of 3 paḻaṅkāśu to provide for burning of a lamp is stated to have been received by an owner of right of worship in the temple, the Śivabrāhmaṇa Āḻvān-dēvagaḷ Suttaṉ alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-bhaṭṭan of Bhāradvāja-gōtra and another a sum of 3 paḻaṅkāśu for a similar purpose was received by Āḷumpirān Uḍaiyapiḷḷai alias Alaitāni-bhaṭṭaṉ and his younger brother Maśakaipukkāṉ of Kavuśika (Kauśika)-gōtra who had agreed to carry out the object of the gift.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiripuvanaccakkaravaṟtikaḷ śrīirājarājatevarkku 3 ṉṟāvatu makaranāyaṟ
ṟup purvapakṣattu saturttiyum mulamum peṟṟa nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaināḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattuk kaḷa
ttūrkkoṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭu tirukkaccūṟkālai mālaipākam vaittāṉ villavāḻvāṉum kaḷariyak
kuḷattuḻāṉ Oṟṟikkoṇṭāṉ Irācarācatevaṉum Uṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiyārkku vaitta ca
ntiviḷakku 2 viḷakku Iraṇṭum Ikkoyiṟ kāṇiyuṭaiya civapaprāmaṇaril bhārattuvāciyāḻvān te
vakaḷ cuttaṉnāṉa nāṟpatteṇṇāyirapaṭṭan kaikkoṇṭa viḷakku 1- kku kaikkoṇṭa paḻaṅkācu 3m kavucikakottira
ttu Āḷumpirān Uṭaiyapiḷḷaiyānalaitānipaṭṭaṉumivan tampi macakaipukkāṉum kaikkoṇṭa cantiviḷakku 1
kkup paḻaṅkācu muṉṟum Āka naṟpaḻaṅkācu 6 Ārum kaikkoṇṭu Ivviḷakku Iraṇṭume cantraātittavarai Erikkak
kaṭavatākac cilālekai paṇṇik kuṭuttom Ivvaṉaivom Itu śrīmāhecora- rakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 26.285 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
